summary: Multi-session attendance feature backend fully verified (8/8 new + 23/23 regression rule-engine tests pass). PUT /employees/{id} persists multi_session_attendance flag; flag-OFF correctly returns 400 'Already punched in/out today'; flag-ON correctly returns 400 'A session is already open' on second concurrent punch-in and 'No open session to punch out' when last session closed; /attendance/my returns sessions[] + session_count + summed hours_worked + first/last mirrored top-level punch_in_time / punch_out_time; late half_day stays half_day even when summed hours >= min (locked); short-hours rule triggers half_day reason=short_hours when sum < min_full_day_hours. Frontend code review: EmployeeEditForm has the 'Allow multiple punch in/out per day' checkbox with data-testid=edit-multi_session_attendance and always-send-boolean save logic; SessionsBadge component renders 'N sessions' badge with data-testid=sessions-badge-{id} and is wired into Attendance.js list rows at two places (my history + team table). Live UI click-through of the Edit modal was not completed: I could not reliably trigger the Edit drawer from the Employees list within the 3-attempt loop limit. Cleanup applied: all TEST attendance for RMF0023 deleted; multi_session_attendance reset to false at end of every test (autouse fixture).
action_items: Main agent self-test the UI: open Employees list -> click eye icon on RMF0023 -> Edit Profile -> tick 'Allow multiple punch in / out per day' -> Save -> reopen and confirm checkbox stays ticked. Then on Attendance page seed a 2-session day and confirm violet 'N sessions' badge appears and expands on hover.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/attendance.py punch_in: late_minutes/auto_status_reason are correctly carried over from the existing record on subsequent sessions (line 174-178) — late half_day will not be downgraded by a later punch-in.
routes/attendance.py punch_out: total_hours sums only sessions with punch_out_time (line 332-335) — open sessions correctly excluded.
routes/attendance.py has a legacy migration path on punch_out (lines 264-283) that synthesises sessions[] from a pre-feature single-session record — good backward compatibility.
routes/employees.py update_employee: multi_session_attendance falls into the generic update_data dict (line 307) and is persisted as-is. Verified via GET roundtrip.
EmployeeEditForm.js save() correctly always-sends booleans (line 81-84) so toggling OFF persists. shift_id-style 'send empty string' is preserved.
SessionsBadge.js correctly hides for sessions.length < 2 — no clutter on single-session days.
Attendance.js: canPunchIn / canPunchOut logic (lines 235-237) correctly uses lastSessionOpen + multiSessionEnabled flag.
Minor: services/shift_rules.py compute_status_after_punch_out_with_shift assumes shift dict — already handled with `(shift or {}).get('min_full_day_hours', 6.0)` so safe.

success_rate.frontend: Code-reviewed only — live click-through of Edit modal not completed

test_credentials: admin/Admin@123 (HR), RMF0023/Welcome@123 (field_agent used as test target)
seed_data_creation: Each test seeds a single attendance_records doc for RMF0023 on today's date and tears it down via autouse cleanup fixture. multi_session_attendance flag on RMF0023 is reset to false after every test. No persistent state changes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-session feature is backend-complete and verified. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_multi_session_attendance.py exercises: (1) flag persistence via PUT/GET /api/employees/{id}, (2) backward-compat 400 'Already punched in/out today' when flag OFF, (3) 'A session is already open' 400 when flag ON and last session unclosed, (4) 'No open session to punch out' 400 when flag ON and last session already closed, (5) /api/attendance/my returns sessions[]/session_count/summed hours_worked/first-punch-in-mirrored top-level fields, (6) late_punch_in half_day lock survives long summed hours, (7) short_hours rule triggers when sum < min. Pattern: hits face-match-skip path indirectly because routes/attendance.py checks for 'already punched' BEFORE running face validation, so emp_h (RMF0023, field_agent role) can trigger the 400 paths without selfie data. For new live punch tests, use a management-role user (skips face match entirely) — none currently exist in db.users so a seed/promotion would be required.
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs found. Test file initially used wrong endpoint paths (/api/punch-in vs /api/attendance/punch-in); fixed in same iteration.
